data.title: User experience could be crypto’s superpower—or its kryptonite
data.description:
Opinion by Jonathan Farnell, CEO of FreedxIt’s 2025, and over 560 million people worldwide are already using cryptocurrency — roughly 17 times the population of Tokyo. That’s a vibrant community, yet for every user who’s embraced it, billions more stand on the sidelines, put off by the complicated interactions and clunky interfaces of protocols, platforms, decentralized apps (DApps), and mobile applications. Why? Blockchain technology offers game-changing potential — decentralized ownership, secure trades — but let’s face it: Most people still find it intimidating, risky, and confusing. User experience (UX) might just be the deciding factor in whether cryptocurrency achieves mass adoption or remains a niche segment
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/user-experience-crypto-s-superpower?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: SEC Explores Registration Exemption for DLT-Based Securities
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Commissioner Hester Peirce has revealed that the SEC's cryptocurrency task force is examining a potential registration exemption. This exemption would allow companies to issue, trade, and settle securities using distributed ledger technology (DLT) while bypassing certain SEC registration requirements. The initiative aims to support businesses in adopting innovative trading systems for eligible tokenized securities.

The proposed exemption comes with stringent conditions to ensure market integrity and prevent fraud and manipulation. Entities benefiting from the exemption would need to provide significant disclosures to users about platform products, services, operations, conflicts of interest, and risks, including those associated with smart contracts. They would also be required to adhere to record-keeping and reporting standards, submit to SEC staff supervision and inspections, and maintain adequate operational financial resources.

Additional requirements for participants offering cryptocurrency custody services may include disclosing custody arrangements and risks to clients, as well as implementing policies and procedures related to blockchain and wallet security. Restrictions on the number and type of tokenized securities listed or traded, as well as trading volume limits, could mitigate risks to investors and the market. For companies that successfully operate under these initial restrictions, the SEC may consider raising these limits.

data.title: Breakout Alert: Ether, Bitcoin Cash-Bitcoin Ratio Break Downtrends as DOGE, SHIB Bottom Out
data.description:
This is a daily technical analysis by CoinDesk analyst and Chartered Market Technician Omkar Godbole.Traders looking for tokens that may see accelerated gains as bitcoin (BTC) rallies might want to focus on ether (ETH) and the ratio between bitcoin cash (BCH) and bitcoin.Both have broken prolonged downtrends alongside bullish bottom formations in leading meme tokens DOGE and SHIB.Ether breakoutEther's price has surged more than 8% today, piercing the trendline (see the left-hand chart) that represents the downtrend from December highs above $4,100

data.title: Virginia Resident Sentenced for Funding ISIS with Cryptocurrency
data.description: According to PANews, the U.S. Department of Justice announced that Mohammed Azharuddin Chhipa, a 35-year-old resident of Virginia, has been sentenced to 30 years and 4 months in prison for financing the terrorist organization ISIS. Evidence presented in court revealed that between October 2019 and October 2022, Chhipa raised funds through social media and traveled hundreds of miles to collect cash. He then converted over $185,000 into cryptocurrency, which was transferred to Turkey and ultimately reached ISIS members in Syria. These funds were used to support ISIS militants and to rescue detained female members. In December 2024, a federal jury found him guilty on five counts, including conspiracy to provide material support to a foreign terrorist organization.

data.title: U.S. Senate to Vote on Updated Stablecoin Regulation Bill
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Senate is scheduled to hold a procedural vote on the updated version of the stablecoin regulation bill, known as the 'GENIUS Act,' at 1:45 PM Eastern Time (1:45 AM UTC+8). The vote follows late-night negotiations and last-minute text updates between Senate Republicans and Democrats, setting the stage for a contentious decision.

Previously reported by BlockBeats, the 'GENIUS Act,' or the 'Guidance and Establishment of National Innovation in U.S. Stablecoins Act,' was introduced in the Senate on February 4, 2025. The legislation aims to establish a comprehensive regulatory framework for 'payment stablecoins' within the United States. Its objectives include promoting financial innovation, protecting consumers, preventing illicit financial activities, and reinforcing the U.S. dollar's dominance in the global financial system.

data.title: Raoul Pal Predicts Bitcoin's Dominance May Have Peaked
data.description: According to Foresight News, former Goldman Sachs executive and Real Vision founder Raoul Pal recently tweeted his belief that Bitcoin's dominance may have reached its peak today. Pal noted the presence of daily, weekly, and monthly DeMark top signals, with the current peak level falling below the highs of 2021, which were themselves lower than the 2017 highs. If this assessment proves accurate, it could signal the onset of the next phase of the 'Banana Zone,' referring to the altcoin season. Pal encouraged observers to watch developments closely.
